heading: Improvements in Coast Guard acquisition management
label: 1102 Improvements in Coast Guard acquisition management

children.heading: .—Level 1 projects
children.label: 1 .—Level 1 projects
children.text: An individual may not be assigned as the project or program manager for a Level 1 acquisition unless the individual holds a Level III acquisition certification as a program manager.

children.heading: .—Level 2 projects
children.label: 2 .—Level 2 projects
children.text: An individual may not be assigned as the project or program manager for a Level 2 acquisition unless the individual holds a Level II acquisition certification as a program manager.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commandant shall designate a sufficient number of positions to be in the Coast Guard’s acquisition workforce to perform acquisition-related functions at Coast Guard headquarters and field activities.

children.label: A
children.text: Program management.

children.label: B
children.text: Systems planning, research, development, engineering, and testing.

children.label: C
children.text: Procurement, including contracting.

children.label: D
children.text: Industrial and contract property management.

children.label: E
children.text: Life-cycle logistics.

children.label: F
children.text: Quality control and assurance.

children.label: G
children.text: Manufacturing and production.

children.label: H
children.text: Business, cost estimating, financial management, and auditing.

children.label: I
children.text: Acquisition education, training, and career development.

children.label: J
children.text: Construction and facilities engineering.

children.label: K
children.text: Testing and evaluation.

children.heading: .—Acquisition management headquarter activities
children.label: 3 .—Acquisition management headquarter activities
children.text: The Commandant shall also designate as positions in the acquisition workforce under paragraph (1) those acquisition-related positions located at Coast Guard headquarters units.

children.heading: .—Appropriate expertise required
children.label: 4 .—Appropriate expertise required
children.text: The Commandant shall ensure that each individual assigned to a position in the acquisition workforce has the appropriate expertise to carry out the responsibilities of that position.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commandant shall establish a management information system capability to improve acquisition workforce management and reporting.

children.label: A
children.text: Qualifications, assignment history, and tenure of those individuals assigned to positions in the acquisition workforce or holding acquisition-related certifications.

children.label: B
children.text: Promotion rates for officers and members of the Coast Guard in the acquisition workforce.

children.heading: .—Appointments to Acquisition Positions
children.label: d .—Appointments to Acquisition Positions
children.text: The Commandant shall ensure that no requirement or preference for officers or members of the Coast Guard is used in the consideration of individuals for positions in the acquisition workforce.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that career paths for officers, members, and employees of the Coast Guard who wish to pursue careers in acquisition are identified in terms of the education, training, experience, and assignments necessary for career progression of those officers, members, and employees to the most senior positions in the acquisition workforce; and

children.label: B
children.text: publish information on such career paths.

children.heading: .—Promotion parity
children.label: 2 .—Promotion parity
children.text: The Commandant shall ensure that promotion parity is established for officers and members of the Coast Guard who have been assigned to the acquisition workforce relative to officers and members who have not been assigned to the acquisition workforce.
